metadata.title: The One Number That Made Insects Meter-Wide
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = high pO2 / giant insects; CRIMSON = modern pO2 / size limit
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no CO2 greenhouse, no Devonian prototaxites, no Triassic recovery, no carbon burial. Wonder register.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: 320 million years ago, you'd share the airspace with dragonfly-like insects the size of seagulls. One number in the atmosphere had climbed high enough to let them sustain bodies that large.

card.copy: The One Number That Made Insects Meter-Wide

build.suggested: suggested prompt: 320 million years ago, you'd share the airspace with dragonfly-like insects the size of seagulls. One number in the atmosphere had climbed high enough to let th

beats.narration_text: Insects breathe through passive tubes called tracheae — oxygen diffuses inward, carbon dioxide diffuses out. No lungs, no heart pumping oxygen — pure diffusion. This sets a hard limit on how large an insect body can be.

production_viz.label: Insect trachea cross-section; O2 diffusing inward; diffusion length limit arrow; size constraint; CRIMSON = limit

beats.narration_text: The key quantity is the partial pressure of oxygen: pO₂ equals f times P, where f is the oxygen fraction and P is total atmospheric pressure. More pO₂ means oxygen diffuses deeper into longer tubes, allowing larger bodies.

card.copy: How does one number control insect size?
card.sub: pO₂ = f × P — partial pressure drives diffusion depth

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The key quantity is the partial pressure of oxygen: pO₂ equals f times P, where f is the oxygen fraction and P is total atmospheric pressure. More pO₂ means oxy

beats.narration_text: Today: f equals 0.21, P equals 1.0 atm, pO₂ equals 0.21 atm. The largest living dragonflies reach about 15 cm wingspan — right at the diffusion limit. In the Carboniferous: f approximately 0.35, P approximately 1.05 atm, pO₂ approximately 0.37 atm — 75% higher than today.

production_viz.label: pO2 dial: today=0.21 atm (CRIMSON=limit), Carboniferous=0.37 atm (TEAL=high); dragonfly size scaling with dial

beats.narration_text: That 75% increase in pO₂ allowed passive diffusion to sustain insect bodies roughly 3 to 4 times larger in cross-section. Griffinflies — order Meganisoptera — achieved 70 cm wingspans — wider than a seagull.

production_viz.label: Griffinfly silhouette (70 cm) vs seagull (60 cm) vs modern dragonfly (15 cm) scale comparison; TEAL=Carboniferous giant

beats.narration_text: Why was oxygen so high? The Carboniferous was the age of vast coal forests — massive plant growth burying carbon. The buried carbon is exactly what we burn today as coal. When plants bury carbon, they leave oxygen behind in the atmosphere.

production_viz.label: Coal forest plants burying carbon; O2 accumulating in atmosphere; coal deposits = buried carbon; TEAL O2 arrow

beats.narration_text: When oxygen fell again at the end of the Carboniferous — as carbon stopped being buried as fast — giant insect sizes collapsed. The correlation between fossil insect size and atmospheric oxygen concentration tracks through geological time.

production_viz.label: Time series: pO2 curve (TEAL) and max insect size curve (TEAL) tracking together; end of Carboniferous collapse

beats.narration_text: You can pressurize modern dragonflies with oxygen-enriched air and breed larger ones over generations — experimentally confirming the pO₂ mechanism. The relationship between oxygen and maximum insect size is not historical speculation; it is mechanistically understood.

production_viz.label: Experiment: modern dragonflies in high-O2 chamber; larger body size over generations; controlled variable = pO2; TEAL confirmation

beats.narration_text: The pO₂ equation matters for humans too. At high altitude, P drops while f stays 0.21 — so pO₂ falls. At 5,500 meters — Everest base camp — P is about 0.5 atm, pO₂ about 0.105 atm — half the sea-level value. The fraction is the same; the pressure delivers less.

production_viz.label: Altitude profile: sea level pO2=0.21, Everest base camp pO2=0.105; same f=0.21, P halved; CRIMSON=altitude sickness zone

beats.narration_text: pO₂ equals f times P. Higher oxygen fraction means deeper diffusion means larger insects. Carboniferous: pO₂ approximately 0.37 atm — 70 cm griffinflies. Today: pO₂ equals 0.21 atm — 15 cm maximum. The atmosphere determined the size of life.

card.copy: pO₂ = f × P controlled insect size
card.sub: Carboniferous 0.37 atm → 70 cm. Today 0.21 atm → 15 cm max.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: pO₂ equals f times P. Higher oxygen fraction means deeper diffusion means larger insects. Carboniferous: pO₂ approximately 0.37 atm — 70 cm griffinflies. Today:

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
